assertions.assertion: Response explains that clients should treat access tokens as opaque and never parse them, since only the resource server (API) should validate access tokens
assertions.rationale: Standard OIDC best practice widely taught

assertions.assertion: Response recommends using the ID token for user identity claims (name, email) in the client application
assertions.rationale: Standard OIDC knowledge about ID tokens vs access tokens

assertions.assertion: Response mentions the userinfo endpoint as an alternative for retrieving user claims
assertions.rationale: Userinfo endpoint is a well-known OIDC endpoint

assertions.assertion: Response explains the distinction: ID tokens prove user identity (consumed by clients), access tokens authorize API calls (consumed by APIs)
